macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 and Dow Hit Fresh Records
items.summary: US stocks closed at record highs on Friday as the S&P 500 gained 0.6% and the Dow rose 0.5%, while the Nasdaq added 0.8%. The December jobs report showed nonfarm payrolls increasing by 50,000 (below expectations) and unemployment falling to 4.4%.

items.title: Chipmakers Lead Market Gains on AI Optimism
items.summary: Semiconductor stocks surged with Broadcom (+3.8%), Intel (+10.8%), and Lam Research (+8.7%) climbing sharply on investor optimism over AI and semiconductor growth.

items.title: Homebuilders Rally on Trump Mortgage Bond Directive
items.summary: Homebuilder and home improvement stocks advanced after President Trump directed mortgage bond purchases to lower rates, with D.R. Horton (+7.8%), PulteGroup (+7.3%), Lennar (+8.8%), and Home Depot (+2.2%) all rising.

items.title: Weekly Market Performance: All Major Indexes Post Solid Gains
items.summary: For the week, the S&P 500 rose 1%, the Nasdaq gained 1.3%, and the Dow advanced 1.2%, driven by strong jobs data and chip stock gains.

items.title: Brazil's Ibovespa Rises on Disinflation Signals
items.summary: The Ibovespa rose 0.3% to 163,370 on Friday as headline inflation eased to 4.26% in December, reinforcing expectations for Central Bank interest rate cuts later in the year.

items.title: FTSE MIB Extends Pullback Amid Tariff Threats
items.summary: The FTSE MIB fell 0.5% to 49,000 on Friday, extending its pullback from record highs as tariff threats and geopolitical tensions weighed on market sentiment.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Solana Breaks $90, Triggering $21M in Short Liquidations
items.summary: Solana (SOL) surged past the $90 resistance level, causing over $21 million in short liquidations and surpassing Bitcoin's $16 million in the last 24 hours. The breakout is supported by the highest ETF inflows since January, with technical indicators suggesting key resistance levels at $110 and $120.

items.title: Bitcoin Holds Above $80K Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin retreated to $79,000-$80,820 range following U.S. military strikes in Iran, triggering $300M in futures liquidations. Crypto futures markets recorded their 67th consecutive day of negative funding rates, the longest streak in a decade.

items.title: CME Group to Launch Bitcoin Volatility Futures on June 1
items.summary: CME Group announced plans to launch bitcoin volatility futures on June 1, enabling investors to bet on BTC price swings pending regulatory approval.

items.title: Core Scientific Sold $208M of Bitcoin in Q1 as AI Pivot Continues
items.summary: Bitcoin miner Core Scientific disclosed it sold $208 million worth of BTC in Q1 2026 while expanding into AI operations, with a landmark 590 MW contract with CoreWeave expected to generate $10.2 billion in revenue over 12 years.

items.title: Trump Media Posts $405M Loss Driven by Crypto Holdings
items.summary: The Trump family media group posted a net drop of $405.9 million in Q1 2026, largely driven by unrealized losses in cryptocurrencies held by the company.

items.title: On-Chain Data Suggests Bitcoin Bear Market May Be Ending
items.summary: Checkonchain's Mean Reversion Index hit a Q10 reading at lows, historically signaling a 90% probability bottom for Bitcoin. Analysts note the February 6 sell-off to $60,000 represented capitulation, though caution remains as traders return to leveraged derivatives markets.

items.title: Swiss Central Bank Bitcoin Reserve Proposal Fails to Secure Signatures
items.summary: A proposal for Switzerland's central bank to hold bitcoin reserves failed to secure enough signatures to amend the nation's constitution, stalling the initiative.

items.title: Quantum Computing Poses $3T Risk to Digital Assets Including Bitcoin
items.summary: Project Eleven warns that quantum computing could endanger $3 trillion in digital assets, including Bitcoin, and pose broader threats to financial security.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: SubQ AI Launches 12 Million Token Context Window Model
items.summary: A new AI startup from Miami released SubQ, a model with a 12 million token context window that can process approximately 120 books in a single prompt at less than 5% the cost of Claude Opus. The model uses sub-quadratic selective attention technology and is reported to be 52 times faster than flash attention at 1 million tokens.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem VR Mod Receives Major Update with Manual Reloading and Full Body Presence
items.summary: A popular VR mod for Resident Evil Requiem is getting a significant update featuring manual reloading mechanics, full body presence, weapon holsters, and performance improvements that bring it closer to a native VR game experience.

items.title: Path of Exile 2 Patch 0.5 Reveals Hidden Content Including Captain America Shield Unique and Atlas Redesign
items.summary: Community analysis of Path of Exile 2's patch 0.5 reveals previously undiscovered details including new unique items, Atlas/monster level difficulty redesigns, and secret ascendancy nodes.

items.title: NBA 2K26 Donovan Mitchell Slasher Guard Build Dominates Random Rec Matches
items.summary: The Donovan Mitchell slasher guard build has become a dominant force in NBA 2K26's random Rec mode, featuring legend-tier badges and contact dunk capabilities.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Chaos erupts as cyberattack disrupts Canvas amid finals
items.summary: A cyberattack has disrupted the learning platform Canvas during finals, causing widespread chaos for students and educators.

items.title: Instructure breach: ShinyHunters claim second hack, deface school websites
items.summary: Hackers ShinyHunters claim a second breach of Instructure, stealing data from nearly 9,000 schools, defacing websites, and locking students out of Canvas with a May 12 negotiation deadline.

items.title: Nintendo raising price of Switch 2 amid chip crisis
items.summary: Nintendo is increasing Switch 2 prices due to chip shortages, after selling 19.86 million units last year but forecasting a sales drop next year.

items.title: Instagram DMs lose end-to-end encryption starting today
items.summary: Meta is shutting down end-to-end encryption for Instagram messaging, removing a key privacy feature from direct messages.

items.title: Apple requires identity verification for Education discounts
items.summary: Apple now mandates identity verification before offering Education Store discounts, including on Apple Watch, to prevent abuse.

items.title: Scientists send unhackable quantum keys across 120 kilometers
items.summary: Researchers demonstrated a stable quantum encryption system using quantum dots, achieving high secure key rates over 120 km of fiber for over six hours.

items.title: Intel signs preliminary deal to produce chips for Apple
items.summary: Intel has reportedly agreed to manufacture chips for Apple, echoing past partnerships amid ongoing supply chain shifts.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Watch can predict fainting 5 minutes in advance
items.summary: Samsung claims its Galaxy Watch can detect impending fainting episodes up to five minutes early using advanced sensors.

items.title: Microsoft's new CPU trick fixes Windows 11 app stutters
items.summary: Microsoft introduces a Low Latency Profile and K2 project to boost Windows 11 performance and eliminate app stuttering issues.

items.title: Sony partners with TSMC for image sensor production
items.summary: Sony is teaming up with TSMC to build a new facility for advanced image sensors.
